questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Inflation reduces the purchasing power of money. Q: Central banks adjust interest rates to control inflation. R: High inflation can erode savings and reduce living standards. S: Moderate inflation is considered healthy for economic growth.

questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Earthquakes occur when tectonic plates shift suddenly. Q: The point of origin is called the epicentre. R: Seismic waves travel through the Earth's crust. S: The Richter scale measures the magnitude of earthquakes.

questions.stem: Find the error: Hardly had (A) / he arrived (B) / than (C) / the meeting started. (D)

questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Biodiversity is essential for ecosystem health. Q: Each species plays a unique role in its habitat. R: Loss of biodiversity threatens ecosystem stability. S: Conservation efforts are crucial to protect endangered species.

R: It emphasises meeting present needs without compromising future generations. S: The UN Sustainable Development Goals outline specific targets.

questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Green Revolution increased agricultural productivity. Q: High-yield crop varieties were developed and introduced. R: Chemical fertilisers and pesticides were used extensively. S: This helped many countries achieve food security.

questions.stem: Find the error: She not only (A) / lost her wallet (B) / but also (C) / her mobile phone. (D)
options: A - should be 'She lost not only'
questions.solution: A - should be 'She lost not only'

questions.stem: Rearrange: P: The Amazon rainforest is often called the lungs of the Earth. Q: It produces about 20% of the world's oxygen. R: Deforestation rates have increased in recent decades. S: International efforts are underway to protect the rainforest.

questions.stem: Find the error: She is (A) / more taller (B) / than her (C) / younger sister. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Each of the participants were given a certificate.
options: Every of the participants were given a certificate.
Each of the participants were given a certificate.
Each of the participants was given a certificate.
Each of the participant was given a certificate.
questions.solution: Each of the participants was given a certificate.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: No sooner he arrived than the meeting started.
options: No sooner did he arrived than the meeting started.
No sooner he had arrived than the meeting started.
No sooner he arrives than the meeting started.
No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.
questions.solution: No sooner had he arrived than the meeting started.

questions.stem: Find the error: He is (A) / better than (B) / any student (C) / in the class. (D)

questions.stem: Correct: The committee have submitted its report.
options: The committee have submitted its report.
The committee has submitted their report.
The committee has submitted its report.
The committee have submitted their report.
questions.solution: The committee has submitted its report.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He is (A) / suffering with (B) / high fever (C) / since last week. (D)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of BENEVOLENCE:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Interleaving:
options: Reorders bits to spread burst errors. Block or convolutional. Used with FEC
questions.solution: Interleaver: scrambles bits. Depth = rows. Burst → random errors after deinterleaving. FEC corrects.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Memory types — RAM:
options: SRAM (static, flip-flop, fast, low density). DRAM (capacitor, slow, high density, refresh needed)
questions.solution: SRAM: 6T cell. DRAM: 1T+1C. DRAM requires refresh (~64ms). SDRAM: synchronous.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PID controller — integral:
options: I = K_i·∫e·dt. Eliminates SSE. May cause instability (adds 90° lag)
questions.solution: I: adds pole at origin. 'Reset' action. Integral windup in saturating systems.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DFT of N-point sequence has N points. For real x[n], DFT X[k] satisfies:
questions.solution: Real signal → Hermitian symmetry: X[k] = X*[(N-k) mod N]. Magnitude even, phase odd.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ampere's law with Maxwell's addition:
questions.solution: Maxwell added displacement current ∂D/∂t. Enables EM wave propagation (no physical current needed).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bipolar junction transistor (BJT) is fundamentally a:
questions.solution: Collector current is controlled by base current (Ic = βIb) — a current-controlled device.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hall effect produces:
options: Transverse voltage perpendicular to current and magnetic field (V_H = R_H·I·B/t)
questions.solution: Hall: V_H = (R_H·I·B)/d. R_H = 1/(nq) for electrons. Determines carrier type, concentration, mobility.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ARM processor — features:
questions.solution: ARM: advanced RISC machine. Used in most mobile SoCs. M-profile for MCU, A-profile for apps.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Active front end (AFE) rectifier: advantage over diode rectifier:
options: Unity power factor and bidirectional power flow
questions.solution: AFE: uses controlled switches (IGBTs). Achieves unity PF, low THD, regenerative capability. More complex.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Overlap-save method: circular convolution output length:
questions.solution: Overlap-save: each block of N produces N valid output samples (after discarding M-1 corrupted samples).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Digital modulation — PSK:
options: Phase shift keying. BPSK (2 phases), QPSK (4 phases). Coherent detection
questions.solution: BPSK: 180° phase shift. QPSK: 90° shifts, 2 bits/symbol. BER BPSK: Q(√(2Eb/N0)).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: BJT in active mode: I_C = β·I_B. For β=100, I_B=20μA:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about an AM modulation index μ is correct?
options: μ should be ≤ 1 to avoid over-modulation
μ has no effect on distortion
μ is the carrier frequency ratio
questions.solution: μ > 1 causes envelope distortion (over-modulation); 0 < μ ≤ 1 is required.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diode rectifier peak inverse voltage (PIV) for full-wave center-tap:
questions.solution: Full-wave center-tap: each diode sees 2V_p when non-conducting.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: PCM quantization: dynamic range increases by:
questions.solution: DR = 6.02n + 1.76 dB for n-bit PCM. Each bit adds ~6 dB. 8-bit → ~50 dB. 16-bit → ~98 dB.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CMOS technology uses:
options: Complementary pairs of NMOS and PMOS transistors
questions.solution: CMOS: low static power (only switching power). NMOS logic: resistor pull-up (static power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Convolutional codes:
options: Encoder with memory (shift register). Viterbi decoding. Used in deep space, LTE
questions.solution: Convolutional: k/n rate, K constraint length. Trellis decoding. Soft decision improves coding gain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MOSFET subthreshold swing:
questions.solution: SS = (kT/q)×ln(10)×(1+C_d/C_ox) ≥ 60 mV/dec. Indicates how sharply device turns off.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FIR filter: linear phase requires:
questions.solution: Linear phase: h[n] = h[N-1-n] (symmetric) or h[n] = -h[N-1-n] (antisymmetric). Constant group delay.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: AM with m=0.3, input SNR=20. Output SNR (envelope detector):

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PN junction capacitance: depletion capacitance decreases with:
questions.solution: C_j ∝ 1/√(V_bi+V_R). Higher reverse V → larger W → lower C_j.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Z-transform of x[n]=a^n u[n]:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 15 kHz and message bandwidth 3 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Doping Si with boron creates:
questions.solution: B has 3 valence electrons. Missing electron → hole → P-type (positive carriers).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Laplace transform of sin(ωt)u(t):

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: QPSK transmits:
questions.solution: QPSK: 4 phases, same BER as BPSK but double data rate. π/4 QPSK, OQPSK variants.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Fermi level in an N-type semiconductor is:
options: Closer to conduction band than valence band
questions.solution: N-type: E_F above intrinsic level (closer to E_C). P-type: E_F below intrinsic (closer to E_V).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transistor as a switch operates in:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A non-inverting op-amp has Rf = 50 kΩ and Rin = 10 kΩ. Its voltage gain is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: State-space: observability matrix O = [C; CA; CA^2; ...; CA^(n-1)]. System is observable if:
questions.solution: Observability: can all states be determined from output? Full rank = yes. Kalman test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Non-inverting: Rf=220kΩ, R₁=4.7kΩ, Vin=3V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lead compensator maximum phase occurs at:
options: Geometric mean of pole and zero frequencies: ω_m=√(z·p)
questions.solution: Lead: ω_m = √(z·p). Max phase φ_m = sin⁻¹((1-α)/(1+α)) where α=p/z.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A link transmits 40 W with a net antenna gain of 10 and a path loss factor of 1000. The received power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FSK modulation:
questions.solution: FSK: f₁ for '0', f₂ for '1'. Minimum Shift Keying (MSK) = continuous phase FSK.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Carson's rule estimates FM bandwidth as:
questions.solution: B_FM ≈ 2(Δf + fm), where Δf is peak deviation and fm the highest message frequency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Routh–Hurwitz criterion is used to determine system stability without:
questions.solution: Routh's array tests sign changes in the first column to count right-half-plane roots directly.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flip-flop — JK:
options: No invalid state. J=K=1 → toggle. J=K=0 → hold
questions.solution: JK: universal flip-flop. J=K=1: Q' = Q (toggle). Race in level-triggered.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Noise figure F = SNR_in/SNR_out. For F=3dB:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Synchronous counter advantage over ripple counter:
options: No propagation delay (all flip-flops clocked simultaneously)
questions.solution: Synchronous: all FFs share clock. No cumulative delay. Faster operation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Race condition:
options: Output depends on order of signal arrival. In latches with feedback. Avoid by edge-triggering
questions.solution: Race: S=R=1 in SR latch → Q and Q' both 1. Level-triggered JK → toggle race. Master-slave avoids.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 4-bit ADC provides how many quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Asynchronous counter:
options: Each FF clocked by previous FF output (ripple counter)
questions.solution: Ripple counter: cumulative delay limits max frequency. Synchronous: faster but more complex.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CAN bus:
options: Controller Area Network. Differential (CAN_H, CAN_L). Priority arbitration. Automotive
questions.solution: CAN: CSMA/CR. 1 Mbps. CRC error checking. Twisted pair. ISO 11898.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NAND gate is universal:
options: Any logic gate can be realized using only NAND gates
questions.solution: NAND and NOR are both universal gates. Can implement AND, OR, NOT using NAND only.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Noise margin:
options: Difference between V_OH(min) and V_IH(min), or V_IL(max) and V_OL(max)
questions.solution: NM_H = V_OH - V_IH. NM_L = V_IL - V_OL. TTL: ~0.4V. CMOS: ~1.5V (at 5V).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ADC parameters:
options: Resolution (bits), sampling rate, quantization error (±½ LSB), SNR = 6.02n + 1.76 dB
questions.solution: Nyquist: f_s ≥ 2·f_max. Oversampling: f_s >> Nyquist. Dither: improve ADC linearity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the logic circuit shown, Y = (A AND B) OR C with A = 1, B = 1, C = 0. The output Y is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: SRAM uses:
questions.solution: SRAM: 6T cell (2 cross-coupled inverters + 2 access transistors). Fast, volatile.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: How many bits are needed to encode 64 distinct quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-bit ADC/DAC provides how many distinct output levels?
questions.solution: n bits quantize into 2ⁿ levels; resolution = full-scale/2ⁿ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Priority encoder converts:
options: Multiple active inputs to coded output (highest priority)
questions.solution: Priority encoder: if multiple inputs active, outputs code of highest priority one.

questions.stem: A link transmits 10 W with a net antenna gain of 10 and a path loss factor of 10000. The received power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Low dropout (LDO) regulator:
options: Dropout < 0.5V. PNP or PMOS pass element. Better efficiency at low dropout
questions.solution: LDO: for battery-powered devices. Quiescent current important. Stable with output capacitor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cellular concept — frequency reuse:
options: Same frequency reused at distance. Reuse factor N (3, 4, 7). Cluster size
questions.solution: Reuse: co-channel interference. D/R = √(3N). Smaller N = higher capacity, more interference.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=47.0kΩ, Rin=22.0kΩ, Vin=0.1V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MMSE equalizer:
options: Minimum mean square error. Balances ISI amplification and noise enhancement
questions.solution: MMSE: W = H*/(|H|² + σ²/σₛ²). ZF: W = 1/H (noises at spectral nulls). MMSE better at low SNR.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amplifier: Rf=220kΩ, Rin=22kΩ. Voltage gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LDPC (Low Density Parity Check):
options: Sparse parity check matrix. Iterative belief propagation. Near Shannon. 5G NR, WiFi, DVB-S2
questions.solution: LDPC: parity check matrix H. Tanner graph. Variable and check nodes. Min-sum algorithm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For an op-amp, the gain–bandwidth product is:
questions.solution: As closed-loop gain rises, bandwidth falls so that gain × bandwidth ≈ constant (unity-gain frequency).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: MOSFET regions:
options: Cutoff, Triode (linear), Saturation (active). Id = (μCox/2)(W/L)(Vgs-Vt)² in saturation
questions.solution: Triode: like resistor. Saturation: current source. Velocity saturation at short channels.

options: High voltage gain, medium input impedance, 180° phase shift
questions.solution: CE: most common. Av = -gm·Rc. Rin = β·re. Rout = Rc.

questions.stem: An ideal operational amplifier has:
options: Infinite open-loop gain and input impedance
questions.solution: Ideal op-amp: infinite gain, infinite Zin, zero Zout, infinite bandwidth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: OFDM:
options: Orthogonal frequency division multiplexing. Multiple subcarriers, cyclic prefix. Used in 4G/5G, WiFi
questions.solution: OFDM: FFT-based. Subcarrier orthogonality. CP eliminates ISI. PAPR issue. LTE: SC-FDMA uplink.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CMOS logic gate consumes power primarily during:
questions.solution: CMOS: near-zero static power. Dynamic power = C·V²·f·α (switching).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Fourier series can represent any periodic signal as a sum of:
questions.solution: A periodic signal decomposes into a fundamental plus its harmonics.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FM is generally preferred over AM for high-fidelity audio because it offers:
questions.solution: FM's constant amplitude makes it far more robust against amplitude noise than AM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Parity bit for error detection can detect:
questions.solution: Parity: odd or even. Detects odd number of bit errors. Cannot correct errors.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Folded cascode op-amp: advantage over telescopic:
questions.solution: Folded cascode: input pair folds into cascode, allows input common-mode near mid-rail. Larger output swing than telescopic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Analog multiplier — Gilbert cell:
options: V_out = K·V_x·V_y. Translinear principle. Four-quadrant multiplication
questions.solution: Gilbert: differential pairs. Balanced modulator. Used in mixers, amplitude modulation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: QPSK symbol rate is:
questions.solution: QPSK: 2 bits/symbol. Symbol rate = bit rate / 2.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Microstrip effective dielectric constant:
questions.solution: εeff = (εr+1)/2 + (εr-1)/2 × correction. Field partly in air, partly in substrate → effective εr between 1 and εr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 555 timer — monostable mode:
options: One pulse per trigger. T = 1.1·R·C. Trigger input < 1/3Vcc
questions.solution: Monostable: timeout period. Retriggerable vs non-retriggerable. Used for debouncing, delay.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amp: Rf=470.0kΩ, Rin=22.0kΩ, Vin=0.5V. Vout:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The decimal equivalent of the binary number 101₂ is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Zener diode regulator:
options: Reverse breakdown. Vout = Vz (constant). Rs limits current
questions.solution: Zener: Vz at Iz. Line regulation: ΔVout/ΔVin. Load regulation: ΔVout/ΔI_L.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An 6-bit ADC provides how many quantization levels?

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: 2-to-4 decoder outputs are:
options: One-hot (only one active at a time)
questions.solution: Decoder: n inputs → 2ⁿ outputs. Exactly one output active (high).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The speed of light in free space:
questions.solution: c = 3×10⁸ m/s. In medium: v = c/√(ε_r μ_r).
